title: Osteospermum clandestinum

intro: Osteospermum clandestinum, also known as the False Blue Eyed Daisy, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in southern Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Osteospermum clandestinum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
